why_it_matters: Product may be seen as final authority or rule-out tool
guardrail: Use support-not-replace language; clinician-owned status; show uncertainty and source provenance

why_it_matters: Closed-loop alerts and red flags can add noise
guardrail: Risk-rank by actionability; local calibration; measure ignored alerts and missed cases

why_it_matters: Normal result may close unresolved symptoms
guardrail: Normal-test explainer and symptom-still-present check-ins; do not mark resolved unless symptom status known

why_it_matters: Repeat visits and patient fear could trigger excessive testing
guardrail: Prompts ask for review/safety net, not mandatory testing; audit utilization and harm
Equity audits; avoid inferred identity; guardrail focuses on physical red flags and coexisting risk

why_it_matters: Language, culture, financial barriers, family history, patient quotes are sensitive
guardrail: Minimum necessary fields; consent; role-based access; patient visibility controls

why_it_matters: More fields/tasks could worsen burnout and documentation burden
guardrail: Summarize; prefill; prioritize; reduce duplicated work; measure time burden
Calm patient-facing language, clear emergency instructions, avoid disease predictions

why_it_matters: Tools may blame CHWs/clinics for unavailable tests/referrals
guardrail: Non-punitive infrastructure reporting; manager escalation; scope-of-practice safeguards

why_it_matters: Patient uploads/OCR and outside records may be wrong
guardrail: Mark unverified; retain original; clinician review; document source/date
